alphaCategories.name: Multi-outcome mutually-exclusive probability distribution
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: GET /events/{event_ticker} (returns sub-markets with yes_bid / yes_ask / no_bid / no_ask / volume / open_interest); GET /markets/{ticker} for per-leg detail
alphaCategories.rationale: For mutually-exclusive events (e.g. next Pope, next UK PM, Xi successor), the live per-outcome implied probability distribution across 5-20 contenders is only available from the real orderbook. A free LLM has no idea which candidate is currently priced at 14c vs 32c on Kalshi right now.
alphaCategories.exampleQuery: What are all candidate outcomes currently listed for 'Who will the next Pope be?' with their YES prices and implied probabilities?
alphaCategories.buyerPersona: Political/event traders and hedge-fund analysts pricing tail-risk bets on succession, election, and IPO-first events who need the full live probability ladder to size positions.

alphaCategories.name: Orderbook depth & liquidity capacity screening
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: GET /markets/{ticker}/orderbook (full depth: yes/no bids and asks with size at each tick) combined with /markets filters
alphaCategories.rationale: Kalshi's website shows top-of-book only. Computing the largest YES position a trader can fill within N cents of slippage requires walking the full bid/ask ladder — a mechanical calculation a free model cannot do without live depth data.
alphaCategories.exampleQuery: Which Economics markets can absorb a $5,000 YES order at ≤ 2c slippage right now?
alphaCategories.buyerPersona: Prop traders and treasury-desk hedgers sizing directional bets where slippage and fill risk materially change P&L.

alphaCategories.name: Time-series price trajectory & re-pricing detection
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: GET /markets/{ticker}/candlesticks and GET /series/{series_ticker}/events/{event_ticker}/candlesticks (OHLC YES-price history with configurable period_interval)
alphaCategories.rationale: Implied-probability drift over the last 7-30 days exposes news-driven re-pricings, mean-reversion opportunities, and momentum. Free LLMs have no intraday time-series of Kalshi contract prices.
alphaCategories.exampleQuery: What was the largest single-day YES price move on 'Will SpaceX land anything successfully on Mars before 2030?' in the last 7 days?
alphaCategories.buyerPersona: Systematic traders running drift/mean-reversion signals and analysts explaining why a market re-priced after a news event.

alphaCategories.name: Within-Kalshi no-arb consistency & mispricing detection
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: GET /events/{event_ticker} sub-markets + GET /markets/{ticker}/orderbook (per-leg best bid/ask); find_arbitrage_opportunities and check_market_efficiency tools
alphaCategories.rationale: Mutually-exclusive Kalshi events must have sum(YES implied probs) = 100c at fair pricing; when YES_ask + NO_ask < 100c on the same leg, there is a literal arbitrage. Free LLMs cannot perform this consistency check without live quotes.
alphaCategories.exampleQuery: Across the sub-markets of 'What will be the largest source of global primary energy consumption in 2030?', does YES-sum = 100c, and if not, where is the mispricing?
alphaCategories.buyerPersona: Quant market-makers and cross-leg arbitrageurs hunting for free edges in Kalshi's mutually-exclusive event ladders.

alphaCategories.name: Volume/open-interest + closeTime recency screening
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: GET /markets with status=open plus volume_24h, liquidity, open_interest, close_time filters; discover_trending_markets and get_markets_by_probability tools
alphaCategories.rationale: Finding actionable shortlists that match multiple thresholds (e.g. 24h vol > $X, probability in [y,z], closing in <14 days) is impossible on Kalshi's UI. This is the raw power of a typed API.
alphaCategories.exampleQuery: Active Elections markets priced 10-40% YES with >$10K 24h volume, ranked by volume.
alphaCategories.buyerPersona: Opportunity scouts and portfolio allocators building a weekly shortlist of decision-relevant bets.

alphaCategories.name: Trade-tape sentiment skew (YES- vs NO-initiated flow)
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: GET /markets/trades with ticker + min_ts (returns taker_side, count, yes_price, no_price per print); analyze_market_sentiment tool
alphaCategories.rationale: Aggregated YES-taker vs NO-taker volume over the last N hours reveals directional conviction beneath the mid — crowd is buying YES even as price stays flat signals impending move. Free LLMs have no trade tape.
alphaCategories.exampleQuery: Over the last 24h, which trillionaire-race contender is seeing the strongest YES-side aggression?
alphaCategories.buyerPersona: Short-horizon tactical traders looking for buy/sell imbalance signals ahead of news.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: What is the current YES price and implied probability on the 'Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?' event on Kalshi?

groundedEntity.title: Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug or resolve_slug resolves KXPERSONPRESMAM-45; get_event returns the sub-market with yes_bid / yes_ask; midpoint = implied probability. Alternatively get_market_orderbook on the primary YES/NO ticker returns best bid/ask directly.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: A free LLM has no knowledge of the current Kalshi YES quote on this event; the price moves with every news cycle about Mamdani's national profile.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: List every candidate currently listed under the 'Who will the next Pope be?' event (KXNEWPOPE-70) on Kalshi with each outcome's YES price and implied probability.

groundedEntity.title: Who will the next Pope be?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event(event_ticker='KXNEWPOPE-70') returns all sub-markets. Each sub-market carries yes_bid/yes_ask/last_price plus ticker/title; implied probability per candidate = yes_ask midpoint (in cents).
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: The candidate ladder and per-name live probabilities on a mutually-exclusive papal race are not in any free model's training data and change as cardinals are discussed.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: What is the current bid-ask spread in cents on the 'Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?' market (event KXOAIANTH-40) on Kalshi?

groundedEntity.title: Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_by_slug('KXOAIANTH-40') → get_market_orderbook(ticker) on the primary sub-market. Spread = best_yes_ask - best_yes_bid in cents, directly from the orderbook response.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Live spread is a function of current orderbook state; free LLMs cannot know whether this market is currently 2c wide or 15c wide.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: List every available market category on Kalshi right now.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_all_categories returns the live category taxonomy (Elections, Politics, Economics, Sports, Financials, Climate and Weather, Science and Technology, Entertainment, Companies, Health, Social, World, Transportation).
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: The category taxonomy can change; relying on training data leads to stale or invented categories. Live categories enable correct downstream screens.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Find all active Kalshi markets in the Elections category with YES implied probability between 10% and 40% and 24h volume above $10,000, ranked by 24h volume.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_markets_by_probability(category='Elections', minProbability=0.10, maxProbability=0.40, minVolume24h=10000) or find_trading_opportunities with the same filters; both wrap GET /markets with status=open and filter fields volume_24h, yes_ask, yes_bid, category.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Multi-criteria screening across the full live Elections book is impossible on the Kalshi UI and impossible for a free LLM without live data.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?' event (KXNEXTUKPM-30), which candidate sub-market currently has the tightest bid-ask spread relative to its midpoint, and what is that spread in cents?

groundedEntity.title: Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXNEXTUKPM-30') returns all candidate sub-markets; get_market_orderbook per ticker returns best yes_bid/yes_ask. Compute spread = yes_ask - yes_bid, midpoint = (yes_bid + yes_ask)/2, then rank by spread/midpoint.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Requires live per-leg orderbook state across all candidates and a ranking computation; not available in Kalshi's UI and impossible for a free LLM.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Using 1-hour candlesticks over the last 7 days, identify the largest single-day YES-price move (in cents) on the 'Will SpaceX land anything successfully on Mars before 2030?' event (KXSPACEXMARS-30).

groundedEntity.title: Will SpaceX land anything successfully on Mars before 2030?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_candlesticks(series_ticker='KXSPACEXMARS', event_ticker='KXSPACEXMARS-30', period_interval=60, start_ts=now-7d). Aggregate hourly OHLC into daily max(high)-min(low); report the day and cent move.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Intraday Kalshi price history is not in any free model; detecting re-pricing windows requires the candlestick endpoint.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Screen the Kalshi Economics category for active markets whose orderbook depth can absorb a $5,000 YES order at ≤ 2 cents of slippage, and list the top 5 by available depth.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: browse_category('Economics') or get_markets(category='Economics', status='open') for candidate tickers; then get_market_orderbook per ticker; walk yes_asks ladder summing size*price until a $5,000 notional is filled and measure top-price vs fill-price slippage in cents. analyze_market_liquidity also wraps this.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Kalshi's UI shows top-of-book only; computing fill-at-slippage across a whole category requires full orderbook depth and automation.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For 'When will any company achieve AGI?' (event KXAGICO-COMP), list each year-bucket sub-market with its current YES price, and report the cumulative implied probability that any company achieves AGI before 2030.

groundedEntity.title: When will any company achieve AGI?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXAGICO-COMP') returns sub-markets where each represents a year bucket (strike). For each sub-market, yes_ask midpoint = implied probability. Sum the midpoints of buckets with strike year <= 2029 to get the cumulative-before-2030 implied probability (since buckets are mutually exclusive).
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Year-bucket AGI timing probabilities are a live structured view that free LLMs cannot produce without the current per-bucket prices.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Compare the current YES prices and orderbook liquidity across the top 5 candidates in the 'Who will be the next new Speaker of the House?' event (KXNEXTSPEAKER-31) on Kalshi, and identify which candidate's pricing looks most out of line relative to its liquidity.

groundedEntity.title: Who will be the next new Speaker of the House?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXNEXTSPEAKER-31') → rank sub-markets by volume_24h, top 5 → get_market_orderbook per ticker. Report yes_ask, cumulative_size_at_top_5c, and flag candidates whose implied probability is high but orderbook is thin (or vice-versa).
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Ranking real candidates by a liquidity-adjusted pricing view requires live orderbooks on every leg; not available from training data or the public UI.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Compare 30-day YES-price trajectories on 'Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?' (KXOAIANTH-40) and 'Will Ramp or Brex IPO first?' (KXRAMPBREX-40). Which event has re-priced more over the last 30 days, in which direction, and by how many cents?

groundedEntity.title: Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event_candlesticks(series_ticker='KXOAIANTH', event_ticker='KXOAIANTH-40', period_interval=1440, start_ts=now-30d) and same for KXRAMPBREX/KXRAMPBREX-40. Compare close[-1] - close[0] for each; larger absolute = more re-priced, sign = direction.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Two IPO-first races diverging over a month reveals which narrative is shifting; requires Kalshi candle history no free LLM has.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Who will be the world's first trillionaire?' event (KXTRILLIONAIRE-30), pick the 3 highest-24h-volume contenders and compare YES-initiated vs NO-initiated taker volume over the last 24 hours. Which contender is seeing the strongest YES-side aggression?

groundedEntity.title: Who will be the world's first trillionaire?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXTRILLIONAIRE-30') → rank sub-markets by volume_24h, top 3 → get_market_trades(ticker, min_ts=now-24h) per ticker (returns taker_side per print). analyze_market_sentiment wraps this. Sum count*price by taker_side; YES-aggression = yes_taker_vol / (yes_taker_vol + no_taker_vol).
mustWinPrompts.alphaCategory: Trade-tape sentiment skew (YES- vs NO-initiated flow)
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Taker-side imbalance on the trade tape surfaces directional conviction before price fully moves — zero chance a free LLM knows this.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Who will be named as Xi Jinping's successor?' event (KXXISUCCESSOR-45JAN01), identify the candidate sub-market that best combines high 24h volume, tight bid-ask spread, and positive 7-day YES-price drift. Which successor is the 'smart-money plus momentum' pick right now?

groundedEntity.title: Who will be named as Xi Jinping's successor?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXXISUCCESSOR-45JAN01') for candidate list with volume_24h; get_market_orderbook per ticker for spread; get_market_candlesticks(ticker, period_interval=1440, start_ts=now-7d) for 7-day close drift. Rank by composite score (volume z-score + spread-tightness z-score + drift z-score).
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: A three-signal synthesis across volume + orderbook + price history on a named candidate ladder is a genuinely premium alpha view.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Using only Kalshi data, find any candidate sub-market inside 'Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?' (KXNEXTUKPM-30) where best YES-ask + best NO-ask is strictly less than 100 cents (implied within-leg arbitrage). Report the leg, the YES-ask, the NO-ask, and the arbitrage edge in cents.

groundedEntity.title: Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXNEXTUKPM-30') for all legs → get_market_orderbook(ticker) each → filter best_yes_ask + best_no_ask < 100. find_arbitrage_opportunities wraps this pattern. Edge = 100 - (yes_ask + no_ask) cents.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Live per-leg arbitrage is mechanical but requires every orderbook read at the same instant — zero chance a free LLM can do this.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For 'How many executive orders will Trump sign in his second term?' (event KXEOTRUMPTERM-29JAN20), compute the market-implied expected value (weighted mean of each bucket's midpoint using current YES prices as probabilities) and report how that expected value has moved over the last 30 days.

groundedEntity.title: How many executive orders will Trump sign in his second term?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXEOTRUMPTERM-29JAN20') returns mutually-exclusive bucket sub-markets with strike ranges and yes_bid/yes_ask. Compute EV_now = sum(bucket_midpoint * yes_ask_prob). Then get_event_candlesticks(series_ticker='KXEOTRUMPTERM', event_ticker='KXEOTRUMPTERM-29JAN20', period_interval=1440, start_ts=now-30d) and re-compute EV per snapshot → delta.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: A single expected-value number from a multi-bucket distribution plus its 30-day drift is a quant-style answer no free LLM can produce without live Kalshi data.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Build a risk-diversified 5-market Kalshi shortlist of actively trading contracts priced between 15% and 40% YES where each market (a) has orderbook depth sufficient for a $2,500 YES position at ≤ 3 cents of slippage and (b) has 7-day cumulative volume ≥ $50K. The shortlist must include at least one market from Elections, one from Economics, and one from Science and Technology.

groundedEntity.title: Elections + Economics + Science and Technology cross-category shortlist

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: browse_category per category; get_markets_by_probability(minProbability=0.15, maxProbability=0.40) to pre-filter; get_market_orderbook per candidate to compute depth at 3c slippage; get_market_candlesticks(period_interval=1440, start_ts=now-7d) to sum 7-day volume. find_trading_opportunities can shortcut the screen.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: A multi-criteria, multi-category screened shortlist is exactly the kind of workflow the upstream UI cannot perform.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Across all sub-markets of 'What will be the largest source of global primary energy consumption in 2030?' (KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30), check whether the sum of best YES-ask implied probabilities equals 100 cents. If it does not, identify which outcome(s) are mispriced and quantify the total edge in cents.

groundedEntity.title: What will be the largest source of global primary energy consumption in 2030?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_event('KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30') returns mutually-exclusive fuel-source sub-markets. get_market_orderbook per leg → collect best yes_ask; sum = implied_total. If implied_total > 100 the book is over-priced (sell YES basket); if < 100 it is under-priced (buy YES basket). check_market_efficiency wraps this.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Mutually-exclusive no-arb consistency check is a mechanical pro-trader analysis requiring simultaneous live quotes across every leg — impossible for a free LLM.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Identify the 3 Kalshi markets closing within the next 14 days with (a) cumulative 30-day volume > $100K and (b) the largest absolute gap between current YES midpoint and the 30-day implied-probability moving average. Report each market's current price, 30-day mean, gap in cents, and direction (rich vs cheap vs mean).

groundedEntity.title: All Kalshi markets closing in ≤ 14 days

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_markets(status='open', max_close_ts=now+14d) for the candidate universe; filter by volume_30d using get_market_candlesticks(period_interval=1440, start_ts=now-30d) aggregated volumes; for each survivor compute mean(close) and |current_mid - mean|; rank by gap desc and return top 3. discover_trending_markets can seed the universe.
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: A 'closing-soon drift shortlist' combining close_time, 30-day volume and 30-day mean-reversion signal is a multi-endpoint workflow the UI cannot do and free LLMs have no data for.

singleVenueToolSynthesis.insight: Converts a mutually-exclusive event into a live candidate-by-candidate probability-and-liquidity table in one pass — essential for any succession / elections / IPO-first race.
singleVenueToolSynthesis.exampleQuery: Show every candidate in 'Who will the next Pope be?' (KXNEWPOPE-70) with YES price, spread, and best-bid size.

singleVenueToolSynthesis.insight: Simultaneous per-leg orderbook reads allow verification of the sum(YES)=100c no-arb constraint and detection of YES_ask+NO_ask<100c intra-leg arbs.
singleVenueToolSynthesis.exampleQuery: Is 'What will be the largest source of global primary energy consumption in 2030?' (KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30) priced consistently across its sub-markets?

singleVenueToolSynthesis.insight: Combines the static outcome distribution with its historical trajectory to compute market-implied expected value now vs 30 days ago for bucket-style events.
singleVenueToolSynthesis.exampleQuery: Has the market-implied expected number of Trump second-term executive orders in KXEOTRUMPTERM-29JAN20 drifted up or down over the last 30 days, and by how much?

singleVenueToolSynthesis.insight: A ranked hot-list filtered by real depth at N cents of slippage and 7-day drift direction gives a directly tradeable shortlist rather than a browse view.
singleVenueToolSynthesis.exampleQuery: Which Elections markets are both trending and absorb a $5K YES order at ≤2c slippage with positive 7-day drift?

singleVenueToolSynthesis.insight: Rolling YES-taker vs NO-taker imbalance across the top 3 legs of a mutually-exclusive event surfaces which outcome is the conviction pick beneath the mid.
singleVenueToolSynthesis.exampleQuery: In 'Who will be the world's first trillionaire?' (KXTRILLIONAIRE-30), which contender is seeing the strongest YES-initiated flow in the last 24h?

upstreamUIGaps: The Kalshi website shows top-of-book bid/ask only; it cannot answer 'how large a position can I fill within 2c of slippage' on any market, which requires walking the full orderbook depth.
The Kalshi UI does not let a user filter markets simultaneously by probability band + 24h-volume threshold + category + days-to-close on a single results page.

There is no Kalshi UI view that checks, across every leg of a mutually-exclusive event, whether the sum of best YES-ask prices equals 100c (no-arb consistency) or quantifies the cent-edge when it does not.

Kalshi's UI does not expose an expected-value number for bucketed multi-outcome events (e.g. executive-order count, unemployment-peak, trillionaire year); users would have to export and hand-compute the weighted mean.
Kalshi does not surface YES-taker vs NO-taker volume imbalance per market over a rolling window; the raw trade tape must be re-aggregated to reveal directional aggression.

There is no UI feature that compares 30-day implied-probability trajectories across two related events (e.g. OpenAI-vs-Anthropic IPO-first vs Ramp-vs-Brex IPO-first) side by side with cent-moves computed.
Kalshi's discovery page does not allow ranking by a composite score (volume z-score + spread-tightness z-score + drift z-score) — that synthesis has to happen client-side via the API.
newAngles: Mutually-exclusive no-arb consistency checks (sum-of-YES legs = 100c) as a first-class alpha signal on multi-candidate Kalshi events.
Market-implied expected-value computation from bucketed numeric events (exec-order count, unemployment-peak, trillionaire year) plus its 30-day drift.
Trade-tape YES-taker/NO-taker aggression ratios on mutually-exclusive event candidates.
Closing-soon drift shortlists: markets closing in ≤14 days ranked by absolute gap between current YES midpoint and their 30-day mean, gated by a 30-day volume floor.
